Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Engineering BAN expression could enable condensed tannin production in forage legumes such as alfalfa for bloat-safe feed, improved nitrogen utilization, reduced greenhouse gas emissions, and modification of flavor/astringency and health-beneficial flavanols in crops.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to the corresponding 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols; e.g., converts cyanidin to (-)-epicatechin, pelargonidin to (-)-epiafzelechin, and delphinidin to epigallocatechin.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: NADPH or NADH required; activity demonstrated with both reduced cofactors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes condensed tannin accumulation and reduces anthocyanin accumulation; ban mutants lose CTs and show precocious red anthocyanins in seed coat.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein enzyme assays in E. coli; HPLC/HPLC-MS/GC-MS/CD structural identification of products; constitutive overexpression in tobacco and Arabidopsis followed by CT and anthocyanin assays.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Seed coat/endothelium during early seed development in Arabidopsis; ectopic expression tested in tobacco flower petals and Arabidopsis leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Recombinant AtBAN and MtBAN converted anthocyanidins to flavan-3-ol products in vitro; no activity in boiled enzyme or empty-vector controls.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Biochemical assays place BAN immediately downstream of ANS; ectopic BAN expression diverts anthocyanidin flux away from anthocyanins toward CTs; ban mutants lose CTs.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Arabidopsis BAN previously identified by transparent testa mutant analysis; Medicago BAN identified by random sequencing of a young developing seed cDNA library and homology to AtBAN.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Arabidopsis ban mutant; transgenic Arabidopsis lines; transgenic tobacco independent lines
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive expression under CaMV 35S promoter in transgenic assays.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC, UV-visible spectrophotometry, HPLC-MS, GC-MS after derivatization, circular dichroism, DMACA staining, butanol-HCl assay, spectrophotometric anthocyanin measurement at 528 nm.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Paper overturns previous suggestion that BAN is leucoanthocyanidin reductase; establishes BAN as anthocyanidin reductase (ANR). Minor ent-catechin-like products likely arise from epimerization artifacts.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Seed cDNA library random sequencing; no accession dataset reported beyond gene accessions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Loss of pink petal pigmentation in tobacco; transparent testa phenotype and precocious red anthocyanin accumulation in Arabidopsis ban mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Condensed tannin / proanthocyanidin branch of flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic expression decreases anthocyanin pigmentation, potentially altering flower/leaf coloration and related traits.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: (-)-Epicatechin and related 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols such as epiafzelechin and epigallocatechin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Anthocyanidins including cyanidin, pelargonidin, and delphinidin
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Transgenic tobacco petals accumulated 7.7–42.7 µg cyanidin equivalents g^-1 FW CTs; transgenic Arabidopsis leaves accumulated 12.9–44.3 µg cyanidin equivalents g^-1 FW CTs; anthocyanin pigmentation reduced in tobacco petals.
Pathway_Genes.Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Medicago truncatula; Nicotiana tabacum
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Seed coat, tobacco flower petals, Arabidopsis leaves

_comments.Species_Latin_Name: 这两个基因不是Nicotiana tabacum的
_comments.Primary_Substrate: Anthocyanidins和2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols不是底物和产物的关系，而是并行的两条代谢途径

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for metabolic engineering of CT accumulation and modification of anthocyanin/CT balance in crops.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts anthocyanidins to corresponding 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols; demonstrated to produce (-)-epicatechin from cyanidin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ectopic expression in Arabidopsis leaves causes CT accumulation; enzyme activity supports CT biosynthesis and reduction of anthocyanin branch flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assay; transgenic Arabidopsis overexpression; product identification by HPLC, MS, and related chemical analyses.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Naturally relevant in developing Arabidopsis seed coat/endothelium at onset of fertilization to preglobular embryo stage; ectopic expression assayed in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Recombinant AtBAN generated identical flavan-3-ol products as MtBAN from anthocyanidins.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Placed immediately downstream of ANS based on in vitro conversion of anthocyanidins and genetic interaction with ans mutant phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously identified through Arabidopsis transparent testa mutant analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Arabidopsis Col-0, ban mutant, transgenic Arabidopsis lines
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC, HPLC-MS, GC-MS, CD, DMACA staining, butanol-HCl assay.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Originally hypothesized to encode LAR, but this paper demonstrates ANR activity instead.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: ban mutant shows transparent testa and red anthocyanin accumulation; overexpression in leaves induces CTs.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Arabidopsis transgenic leaves contained 12.9–44.3 µg cyanidin equivalents g^-1 FW CTs.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Strong candidate for engineering condensed tannin accumulation into forage legumes and other crops because its ectopic expression generates CTs in heterologous tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols, including cyanidin to (-)-epicatechin and pelargonidin to (-)-epiafzelechin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive expression in tobacco petals eliminates pink anthocyanin pigmentation and causes CT accumulation; recombinant enzyme forms epicatechin/epiafzelechin products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning from seed library; RNA expression profiling; recombinant E. coli enzyme assays; transgenic tobacco overexpression; CT and anthocyanin quantification; product structural analyses.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Highly expressed in young developing seeds of Medicago truncatula; weak in flowers and very weak in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Purified recombinant MtBAN converted cyanidin, pelargonidin, and delphinidin to corresponding cis-flavan-3-ols with cofactor dependence.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts immediately after ANS, using anthocyanidins as substrates; ectopic expression shifts products from anthocyanins to CTs.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Random sequencing of a cDNA library from young developing seeds followed by sequence homology analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: 36 independent transgenic tobacco lines; Medicago truncatula seed cDNA source
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: RNA gel blot; HPLC; UV-visible spectrophotometry; HPLC-MS; GC-MS; NMR; circular dichroism; DMACA staining; butanol-HCl assay.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Exists as a single copy in M. truncatula genome. The paper provides detailed stereochemical assignment of product (-)-epiafzelechin.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Anthocyanin depletion in petals may affect coloration-related traits.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Tobacco petals accumulated 7.7–42.7 µg cyanidin equivalents g^-1 FW CTs in transgenic lines; petals showed reduced anthocyanin absorbance at 528 nm.
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Accumulation/content and branch allocation versus anthocyanins

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthesis upstream of condensed tannin branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Provides precursor supply for BAN/ANR-dependent CT formation, but not the focal engineering target in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols such as dihydroquercetin to flavan-3,4-diols (leucoanthocyanidins).
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Likely NADPH-dependent as canonical DFR; specific cofactor not emphasized here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies leucoanthocyanidin precursor in coupled assays used to test BAN; participates in flavonoid pathway leading toward anthocyanins and CTs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant M. truncatula DFR used in coupled enzymatic reactions with BAN.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Assayed in vitro in coupled reaction with MtBAN and dihydroquercetin.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Used as recombinant enzyme in coupled assays generating flavan-3,4-diol substrate for BAN testing.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Known upstream flavonoid enzyme; in this study used to generate precursor before the BAN step.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Not discovered in this paper; used as known pathway enzyme/candidate comparator.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Mentioned largely for comparison because BAN shares sequence similarity with DFR.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Important for balancing anthocyanin versus CT production; ans mutants reduce CT accumulation, informing pathway engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase; requires 2-oxoglutarate and likely Fe2+/O2/asorbate as canonical co-substrates/cofactors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for normal CT accumulation because BAN acts immediately downstream on anthocyanidins; ANS mutants show greatly reduced CTs and transparent seed testa.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic evidence from Arabidopsis ans mutants (tt18/tt78) stained with DMACA showing greatly reduced CT levels; pathway logic integrated with BAN biochemical assays.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known flavonoid enzyme assignment; mutants lacking ANS activity are referenced and used here.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: BAN biochemical data place ANS immediately upstream by generating anthocyanidin substrate; ans mutant has reduced downstream CTs.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Arabidopsis tt18/tt78 mutant and Col-0 control
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: DMACA staining for CT; mutant phenotype observation.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The text refers to ANS mutant tt18 and figure legend mentions tt78 lacking ANS activity; both indicate reduced CT accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Transparent testa phenotype due to loss of seed coat pigmentation-related metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter both anthocyanin pigmentation and CT content.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: CTs greatly reduced in ans mutant by DMACA staining; no explicit numeric value given.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target for improving correct condensed tannin accumulation/storage in seeds or engineered tissues, though not functionally dissected here.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Implicated in Arabidopsis seeds for correct CT accumulation; temporal/spatial requirements suggested during seed development.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for correct condensed tannin accumulation in Arabidopsis seeds.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic evidence from prior studies cited in this paper; no direct functional assay performed in this report.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Accessory factor required for proper CT accumulation rather than core catalytic step.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Seed development, particularly endothelium/seed coat context where CTs accumulate.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously identified genetically as a transparent testa-related factor necessary for CT accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Arabidopsis seed mutants from prior genetic studies
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Mentioned alongside BAN and several transcription factors as factors necessary for proper CT accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transport/sequestration associated with condensed tannin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Genetic phenotype and CT accumulation assays in cited prior work; no assay newly reported here.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Described as a multidrug transporter-like protein, so categorized here as a non-enzymatic regulatory/transport-related factor rather than a pathway enzyme. Evidence in this paper is indirect and literature-based.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Transparent testa-related seed coat phenotype likely in prior studies.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may affect vacuolar transport or other flavonoid-related compounds.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Likely flavonoid/CT transport or sequestration process; direct downstream targets not defined in this paper.

Title: Role of Anthocyanidin Reductase, Encoded by BANYULS in Plant Flavonoid Biosynthesis
